codeReview.summary: The feature appears to have achieved its scoped goal: it removed eager rune-store imports from Vitest-triggered paths by introducing lazy bridge modules for editor/project side effects and by extracting assistant tool types into non-rune modules. The targeted blocked suites cited in the handoff now load in plain Vitest contexts, and the remaining full-suite failure was explicitly a separate ProblemsView baseline issue already handled by a later feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the stability-hardening procedure, but the mission handoff omitted the reviewed feature's commit ID even though the review workflow depends on it. That forced review to fall back to working-tree diff evidence instead of the documented `git show <commitId>` path.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission handoff `C:\Users\User\.factory\missions\a751629e-aa17-4ca2-8216-dab17f68a6ab\handoffs\2026-03-25T06-34-28-917Z__stabilize-vitest-rune-store-import-baseline__f229c707-c24a-4270-b1ba-1b61f1691451.json` has no top-level `commitId`; the transcript skeleton also ends with `successState: "partial"` and `git status --short` instead of a commit step.

summary: Reviewed the feature transcript skeleton, handoff, current diff evidence, skill instructions, and shared-state docs. The implementation plausibly fixes the targeted `rune_outside_svelte` baseline by lazily importing editor/MCP/project-store dependencies and extracting non-rune types, and I found no blocking code issue in scope. The main review gap is process/shared-state related: the handoff did not record a feature commit ID, so future scrutiny should ensure commit metadata is captured when review instructions require commit-based diff inspection.
